description_long: This object is a historical field cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with large spoked wheels, set atop a stone platform. The cannon barrel is made of dark, weathered metal, featuring reinforcing bands and a flared muzzle. The wooden carriage is reinforced with metal brackets and bolts, and the wheels are robust with visible iron rims. The stone base is rectangular with a slightly raised, rounded front section, and a small pile of cannonballs is placed in front of the muzzle. The overall appearance suggests a 19th-century artillery piece, likely used in military fortifications or urban defense. The materials and construction details evoke a sense of age and durability, with visible wear and patina on both wood and metal surfaces.
description: A weathered historical field cannon on a wooden carriage with large spoked wheels, mounted on a stone platform with cannonballs in front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cannon barrel's muzzle, the split wooden carriage arms, and the stone platform beneath.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the full length of the cannon barrel, the wooden carriage, and the large spoked wheel.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the breech of the cannon, both wheels, and a pile of cannonballs on the stone platform.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the opposite side of the carriage, the wheel, and the full length of the cannon barrel.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on a wooden carriage.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; surfaces are solid brown and gray with natural weathering.
